assertions.description: The overflow-anchor CSS property is supported (the spec hook for opting in/out of scroll anchoring).
overflow-anchor: none is supported for opting out of scroll anchoring on a subtree.
document.activeElement is exposed; the priority candidate algorithm uses focused-editable as a candidate (one of the inputs to the fix).
::target-text or find-in-page highlight rendering uses the search-results pseudo. The priority candidate fix mediates between this and editable focus.
Element.prototype.scrollTo exists — programmatic scrolls remain unaffected; only the anchoring candidate priorities changed.
scrollend event is exposed; scroll anchoring corrections still flow through normal scroll lifecycle.
